name: Idefics2 8B
archDisplay: SigLIP-SO400M vision encoder + Mistral-7B-v0.1 decoder, connected via learned Perceiver resampler + MLP modality projection
note: Compact Apache-2.0 VLM roughly 10x smaller than IDEFICS-80B, with strong OCR/document QA via SigLIP + Mistral-7B.
tpsNote: llama.cpp only converts the Mistral-7B LLM portion (mmproj vision tower unsupported), so no verified end-to-end Q4 4090 throughput exists.
verificationNotes: Confirmed real April 15 2024 Hugging Face release, Apache-2.0, 8B params (SigLIP-SO400M + Mistral-7B-v0.1), 32K/8K-sliding-window context inherited from Mistral-7B-v0.1 — all verified against the official blog. CORRECTED: the source blog publishes two parallel rows ('w/o image splitting' vs 'w/ image splitting'); the original record's MMMU (43.5) and MathVista (51.6) values actually belonged to the 'w/o image splitting' row while TextVQA/DocVQA/MMBench/VQAv2 belonged to the 'w/ image splitting' row — an inconsistent mix. Corrected MMMU to 43.0 and MathVista to 51.4 to match the 'with image splitting' row consistently with the other 4 (already-correct) rows, since that configuration is also the one that matches the note's claim of 'strong OCR/document QA' (74.0 DocVQA vs. 67.3 without splitting).
